aliases: 韩式紫菜包饭 (Hán Shì Zǐ Cài Bāo Fàn)

chinese: 韩式紫菜包饭 (Hán Shì Zǐ Cài Bāo Fàn)

food_subgroup: Sesame-seasoned short-grain white rice rolled in nori (dried seaweed) with pickled daikon, imitation crab (surimi), spinach, carrots, and egg; 1 full roll (~220g, 8 pieces)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn does not have a dedicated gimbap entry; FOODON_03301504 (rice roll, seaweed) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A003H (seaweed rice roll) used as closest parent; no dedicated gimbap code exists in FoodEx2 as of 2024.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code for gimbap or seaweed rice roll identified in the SNOMED CT international release.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is one full gimbap roll (~220g), sliced into 8 equal pieces, providing approximately 350 kcal, 10g protein, 8g fat, and 52g carbohydrate.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Gimbap / Korean seaweed rice roll (SR Legacy composite)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for cooked short-grain white rice, nori, pickled daikon, imitation crab (surimi), cooked spinach, cooked carrots, scrambled egg, and sesame oil, combined in proportions consistent with a standard Korean gimbap roll. Values cross-referenced against the Korean Nutrition Database (KNDB) via USDA SR Legacy composite. Per-100g figures are consistent with the input record (energy 159 kcal, protein 4.5g, fat 3.6g, carbohydrate 23.6g) and are retained as verified.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values — white rice analogous estimate; sesame oil fat-coating effect on rice GI documented in peer-reviewed literature
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care 2008
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for gimbap exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI ~58 is estimated from the closest analogous food: sesame oil-seasoned short-grain white rice. Plain cooked white rice (short-grain) has a GI of approximately 72 (University of Sydney GI Database). The sesame oil coating of rice grains in gimbap preparation creates a thin lipid barrier that slows starch gelatinisation and digestion, reducing GI by approximately 10–15 units relative to plain white rice, consistent with published fat-starch interaction data (Liljeberg & Bjorck, 1998, AJCN). GI ~58 (medium) is therefore a defensible analogous estimate. GL per full roll (220g): 23.6g carb/100g × 2.2 × 58/100 = ~30.2.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content ~750mg per full roll (341mg/100g × 2.2), primarily from pickled daikon, imitation crab surimi, and sesame-soy rice seasoning
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should limit gimbap to a half-roll (4 pieces, ~110g, ~375mg sodium) as a snack, or account for the full-roll sodium load (~750mg, approximately 33% of the 2300mg daily limit) within their total daily sodium budget.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: GI ~58 (medium), GL ~30.2 per full roll; carbohydrate load of ~52g per roll from sesame-seasoned short-grain white rice
type2_diabetes.guidance: Patients with Type 2 Diabetes should consider limiting intake to a half-roll (4 pieces, ~110g, GL ~15.1) as a snack portion; if consuming a full roll as a meal replacement, pair with additional non-starchy vegetables or protein to blunt the postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (0.7g/100g, ~1.5g per roll), trans fat 0g, moderate sodium (~750mg per roll), and anti-inflammatory sesame oil (ALA omega-3, lignans)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Gimbap is a relatively heart-friendly Korean food with low saturated fat and no trans fat; however, patients with cardiovascular disease should monitor sodium intake and prefer a half-roll serving to keep sodium below 400mg per eating occasion.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Anti-inflammatory components: sesame oil (ALA, lignans), spinach (folate, vitamin A), carrots (beta-carotene); no major pro-inflammatory ingredients
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Gimbap is suitable for patients with rheumatoid arthritis; sesame oil, spinach, and carrots provide anti-inflammatory micronutrients, and the moderate sodium level (~750mg per roll) is manageable within an anti-inflammatory dietary pattern.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Gimbap (김밥, also romanized as Kimbap) is South Korea's most popular portable meal — a roll of sesame oil-seasoned short-grain white rice wrapped tightly in a sheet of dried nori seaweed, filled with pickled daikon radish, imitation crab (cooked surimi), blanched spinach, julienned carrots, and pan-fried egg strips. The roll is sliced into 8 equal bite-sized pieces. Unlike Japanese sushi maki, gimbap rice is seasoned with sesame oil and salt (savoury, nutty flavour) rather than rice vinegar (sweet-sour), and gimbap never contains raw fish. Per full roll (~220g, 8 pieces): approximately 350 kcal, 10g protein, 8g fat, 52g carbohydrate, GI ~58 (medium), GL ~30.2, and ~750mg sodium. Per 100g: 159 kcal, 4.5g protein, 3.6g fat, 23.6g carbohydrate, 341mg sodium. The sesame oil coating of the rice grains lowers the GI relative to plain white rice (~72) by slowing starch digestion. Gimbap is a staple of Korean school lunches, picnics, and convenience stores. It is widely available in Singapore at Korean grocery stores and Korean restaurants. Clinically: moderate glycaemic load per full roll; moderate sodium; low saturated fat; suitable for most adults in portion-controlled servings.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are consistent with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy composite data for gimbap component ingredients and are cross-referenced against the KNDB via USDA. No single exact USDA FoodData Central entry for gimbap exists; values are adapted from a component composite. GI is an analogous estimate from sesame-seasoned white rice, not a direct measurement. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for gimbap as a composite dish is not available as a single entry in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy or the Singapore HPB database as of 2024; individual component fatty acid data could be summed but would introduce estimation uncertainty beyond the scope of this record. water_g set to -1: moisture content for the assembled gimbap roll is not reported as a single value in USDA FoodData Central for this composite preparation. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central component data; no hydrogenated fats are used in standard gimbap preparation. GI ~58 is an analogous estimate based on sesame oil-seasoned short-grain white rice; no direct GI measurement for gimbap exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GL per full roll calculated as: (23.6g carb/100g × 220g / 100) × 58 / 100 = 30.2. Sodium per full roll ~750mg (341mg/100g × 2.2). The input record description references 'marinated beef' but the ingredient list and nutritional profile are consistent with the standard gimbap formulation (pickled radish, imitation crab, spinach, carrots, egg, sesame rice); the beef description has been treated as a description error and the standard formulation is used.
